rule_delta.description: Bishops move orthogonally only — horizontally or vertically any number of squares. Diagonal movement is illegal.
rule_delta.geometric_description: One piece type may only travel in straight lines — left, right, forward, or backward — any number of empty squares. It cannot travel diagonally under any circumstance.
rule_delta.stacked_description: Each player makes exactly two consecutive moves per turn before the opponent replies.
rule_delta.stacked_geometric_description: Each side executes two actions before control passes to the opponent.

1. Bishops move orthogonally only — horizontally or vertically any number of squares. Diagonal movement is illegal.
2. Each player makes exactly two consecutive moves per turn before the opponent replies.

Current position (FEN): 3r2k1/pQ3pp1/1b5p/8/8/2P2NP1/qP3PKP/5R2 w - - 0 23
It is White to move.

Provide a sequence of exactly 3 moves in UCI notation, one per line,
applying all modified rules consistently across every move.
Then answer the reflection questions honestly — your reflection is scored
independently of your moves.

MOVES (one per line):
[move 1]
[move 2]
[move 3]

CONFIDENCE: [1-10, how certain you are ALL 3 moves follow the modified rules]
LEGAL_PREDICTION: [YES or NO — do you believe all 3 moves are legal under the modified rules?]
HARDEST_PART: [one sentence — which rule or move in the sequence was hardest to apply and why]

rule_delta.description: Knights move exactly 2 squares in any direction — horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. The standard L-shape is illegal.
rule_delta.geometric_description: One piece type may move exactly 2 squares in any direction — horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. No other distance or path is permitted.
rule_delta.stacked_description: A player cannot move the same piece that was moved on their previous turn.
rule_delta.stacked_geometric_description: The piece moved on the current turn must differ from the piece moved on the immediately preceding turn.

1. Knights move exactly 2 squares in any direction — horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. The standard L-shape is illegal.
2. A player cannot move the same piece that was moved on their previous turn.

Current position (FEN): r5r1/pp5k/3R3p/8/8/2P1P3/PP3PPP/2KR4 w - - 3 24
It is White to move.

Provide a sequence of exactly 3 moves in UCI notation, one per line,
applying all modified rules consistently across every move.
Then answer the reflection questions honestly — your reflection is scored
independently of your moves.

MOVES (one per line):
[move 1]
[move 2]
[move 3]

CONFIDENCE: [1-10, how certain you are ALL 3 moves follow the modified rules]
LEGAL_PREDICTION: [YES or NO — do you believe all 3 moves are legal under the modified rules?]
HARDEST_PART: [one sentence — which rule or move in the sequence was hardest to apply and why]

rule_delta.description: The queen cannot move toward its own back rank. Only forward and sideways movement is allowed.
rule_delta.geometric_description: One piece type may not move in the direction of its own starting rank. It may only move laterally or away from its starting rank.
rule_delta.stacked_description: The first player to capture all opponent pawns wins, regardless of check or checkmate.
rule_delta.stacked_geometric_description: Victory is achieved by removing all of the opponent's smallest pieces from the board.
